title: Bounded Worker Pool, Origin Fairness, Cancellation, and Shutdown · Guided Systems Studio
summary: A public-interest newsroom incrementally builds bounded origin-aware ingestion worker pool from original RSS, Atom, HTTP, and PostgreSQL fixtures while retaining prior Go, HTTP-client, HTTP-server, SQL, Git, accessibility, security, testing, cancellation, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of bounded origin-aware ingestion worker pool while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practi…
objectives: Derive global, tenant, origin, database, memory, and queue budgets and enforce the minimum active constraint.
Assign producers, closers, receivers, buffers, and terminal states so sends, closes, and drains cannot race or block forever.
Prevent one publisher from consuming all slots while preserving useful throughput and explicit per-origin limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Concurrency Budget
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go team handles case 3420 during a publisher-fairness incident rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove bounded origin-aware worker ownership, failure isolation, cancellation, and joined shutdown; the release claim must bind source, migrations, dependencies, binary, image, configuration, and restored state; then change one schema, constraint, transaction, upsert, migration, query, or search plan and preserve admitted state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Derive global, tenant, origin, database, memory, and queue budgets and enforce the minimum active constraint.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Concurrency Budget.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for derive global, tenant, origin, database, memory, and queue budgets and enforce the minimum active constraint. For Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Concurrency Budget, keep Feed Go Concurrency Budget connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.
